subject: Mathematics
title: Newton: Gravity and Calculus
description: A quick, practical guide to Newton: Gravity and Calculus.

hook.text: In 1665, plague closed Cambridge. Isaac Newton went home and invented calculus in his spare time.

buildup.text: He also discovered the law of gravity, split white light into colors, and built a reflecting telescope.

discovery.text: His calculus let scientists describe motion, growth, and change with precise equations.

twist.text: He kept his work secret for decades, sparking a bitter priority war with Leibniz.

climax.text: His Principia Mathematica is considered the most important scientific book ever published.

punchline.text: A plague year produced the most productive mind in history.

quiz.question: During which event did Newton develop much of his groundbreaking work?
